coaching.coaching: Pin down AUTHOR_GUIDES entry value structure with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: AUTHOR_GUIDES entry value structure

coaching.coaching: Pin down explain_criterion rendering protocol under new approach with a concrete worked example.

coaching.coaching: Test the riskiest assumption behind descoped dev/CI machinery compatibility assumption first.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the post-migration type and example value of AUTHOR_GUIDES entries (e.g. `dict[str, str]` with values as bare filenames like `'writing-a-passing-plan.md'`) so the lookup protocol in `explain_guide` and the enum definition are co-specified.
evidence: Current codebase: AUTHOR_GUIDES: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = { 'plan': ('docs', 'writing-a-passing-plan.md'), 'review': ('docs', 'passing-code-review.md') }
Plan says: "explain_guide(name) resolves via resources.files('rebar._guides') / relname" — implies relname is a plain string, not a tuple
AUTHOR_GUIDES type annotation and its consumers (explain_guide, _cli/_llm_commands.py) are NOT updated by the plan's spec; the mismatch is implicit
findings.finding: The plan says AUTHOR_GUIDES 'maps name -> filename' under importlib.resources, but never defines the new value structure of AUTHOR_GUIDES entries. The current codebase stores values as tuple[str, ...] of path components (e.g. ('docs', 'writing-a-passing-plan.md')); the new resources.files('rebar._guides') / relname pattern requires a plain filename string. An executor implementing the migration has no spec for what AUTHOR_GUIDES values look like post-migration, creating a structural gap between the enum definition and the lookup protocol that consumes it.
findings.location: Approach section, step 2: "AUTHOR_GUIDES maps name -> filename"
scenarios: Executor keeps AUTHOR_GUIDES as tuple[str, ...] and passes the tuple to resources.files() / operator, causing a TypeError at runtime
Executor changes AUTHOR_GUIDES to dict[str, str] but leaves the existing config.repo_root(...).joinpath(*relpath) callsite in explain_guide untouched, breaking the old path unpacking
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach step 2: 'AUTHOR_GUIDES changes type to dict[str, str]: { "plan": "writing-a-passing-plan.md", "review": "passing-code-review.md" }; explain_guide resolves via resources.files("rebar._guides") / AUTHOR_GUIDES[name].'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the complete new processing protocol for explain_criterion under step 3: how it retrieves the full criterion dict from load_criteria output, how it calls _guide_section_body on it, and whether the missing-file error kind is retired from explain_criterion's contract (updating the MCP docstring and tests accordingly).
evidence: Current explain_criterion: ids = {c['id'] for c in load_criteria(...)} — discards the full dicts, only keeps IDs
_guide_section_body signature: def _guide_section_body(criterion: dict[str, Any]) -> str — needs the full criterion dict, not just an ID
Plan text: 'renders the section directly from the packaged registry via the existing _guide_section_body' — no join protocol specified
The missing-file error kind is currently documented in the MCP docstring as part of explain_criterion's contract (kind ∈ unknown-id / malformed-registry / missing-file)
findings.finding: Approach step 3 says explain_criterion will 'render the section directly from the packaged registry via the existing _guide_section_body', but the new processing protocol is not specified: _guide_section_body(criterion_dict) takes a full criterion dict, while explain_criterion currently only tracks criterion IDs from load_criteria and then reads a pre-rendered file. The plan does not specify (a) how explain_criterion locates the matching criterion dict (not just its ID) from load_criteria output, (b) whether it calls load_criteria a second time or restructures the first call, or (c) what fallback/error kind applies if load_criteria returns the ID but _guide_section_body produces empty output.
scenarios: Executor restructures explain_criterion to call load_criteria and find the matching dict, but the fallback for a criterion present in IDs but absent from the full dict list (e.g. overlay shadowing) is unspecified
After migration, explain_criterion can never raise missing-file, but MCP callers and tests that expect that kind are not updated — silent behavioral regression

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `validate_criteria_guide` remains meaningful after `explain_criterion` bypasses the guide file: either update it to cross-check `_guide_section_body` output against the generated file, or document that it becomes a structure-only gate and accept that content divergence is undetected.
evidence: registry.py lines 543-545: _GUIDE_RELPATH = ('docs', 'plan-review-criteria-guide.md') — the guide file that explain_criterion currently reads from
registry.py lines 612-628: validate_criteria_guide() reads from _guide_path() which calls config.repo_root(); this is unchanged per the plan's scope exclusion
registry.py lines 631-653: explain_criterion() currently reads the guide file via _guide_path; the plan changes this to use _guide_section_body instead
registry.py lines 699-701: _main() calls validate_packaged_routing() + validate_criteria_guide() — the CI parity gate depends on the guide file remaining accurate

If explain_criterion no longer reads the guide file, validate_criteria_guide becomes a gate on a file that nobody consumes at runtime — divergence between the file and _guide_section_body output would be invisible to users.
findings.finding: The plan's scope exclusion 'Keep the dev/CI machinery (regenerate_criteria_guide, validate_criteria_guide, _guide_path, coach deep-links) unchanged' is an unverified descoping claim that may silently delete necessary reconciliation work. After the plan is implemented, explain_criterion reads criterion sections directly from the packaged registry via _guide_section_body (no file I/O, repo-root-independent), but validate_criteria_guide still reads from _guide_path (repo-root-dependent, the generated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md). These are now two independent representations of the same content: the file parity gate in CI would check a file that the runtime explain_criterion no longer uses. If the file drifts (e.g. _guide_section_body output changes but regenerate was not re-run), the CI gate is green but the installed explain output is different — making the gate meaningless. The plan does not address whether validate_criteria_guide remains a meaningful gate post-change, or whether it must be updated to cross-check _guide_section_body output directly.
findings.location: Approach §3 and §5: 'explain_criterion(id) renders the section directly from the packaged registry via the existing _guide_section_body' / 'Keep the dev/CI machinery (regenerate_criteria_guide, validate_criteria_guide, _guide_path, coach deep-links to the docs anchor) unchanged.'
scenarios: A criterion's scenario/checklist fields change in the packaged registry (criteria_routing.json), _guide_section_body returns updated text, but regenerate_criteria_guide was not re-run — validate_criteria_guide passes (the file still has old sections matching CANONICAL_LLM ids) but rebar explain returns different content than the guide file, with no CI gate catching the drift.

An installed rebar user runs rebar explain F1 and gets output derived from _guide_section_body, but the docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md file (if present) has different content — there is no reconciliation check to catch this.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or approach note: after the switch, update `validate_criteria_guide` to also assert that each section's content matches `_guide_section_body(criterion)` — so the guide file stays a faithful mirror of what `explain_criterion` would return, and the parity gate stays meaningful.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name `test_explain_criterion_missing_file`, `test_explain_cli_missing_and_malformed`, `test_explain_mcp_success_and_error_states`, and `test_explain_guide_missing_file` in the plan as tests requiring mechanical update (not just 'explain test suite pass').
evidence: `test_explain_criterion_missing_file` (line 62): expects `missing-file` from `explain_criterion('F1', repo_root_path=str(tmp_path))` — after moving to `_guide_section_body`, no file lookup occurs, so this never raises.
`test_explain_cli_missing_and_malformed` (line 89–93): sets `REBAR_ROOT=tmp_path`, expects exit 1 (missing-file) for `explain F1` — same breakage.

`test_explain_mcp_success_and_error_states` (line 111–112): sets `REBAR_ROOT=tmp_path`, calls `explain_criterion tool('F1')`, expects `kind == 'missing-file'` — no longer reachable for criterion lookups after the change.

`test_explain_guide_missing_file` (line 135): passes `repo_root_path=str(tmp_path)` to `explain_guide` — after `importlib.resources` resolution, `repo_root_path` is irrelevant to guide loading, so the call succeeds instead of raising.
findings.finding: Three existing test functions that assert `missing-file` behavior for `explain_guide` and `explain_criterion` under repo-root isolation become incorrect after the change, but the plan does not enumerate them as specific update targets.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria bullet 5 ('tests updated to assert repo-root independence'); tests/unit/test_plan_review_explain.py lines 62–66, 89–93, 111–112, 135–138
scenarios: Tests silently pass false-positively if `missing-file` can no longer be triggered and the assertion is removed but the positive path is not added.
Tests fail at merge if not updated, blocking CI.
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC or Approach, add: 'Update `test_explain_criterion_missing_file` and `test_explain_cli_missing_and_malformed` to verify the criterion path now succeeds under a bare tmp_path (no missing-file). Update `test_explain_guide_missing_file` to drop the `repo_root_path` isolation expectation and assert the guide is accessible from any root. Update `test_explain_mcp_success_and_error_states` to remove the `REBAR_ROOT=tmp_path → missing-file` assertion for criterion IDs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command (or `pytest` selector) to each AC — at minimum: (AC1) `REBAR_ROOT=/tmp/empty rebar explain plan && rebar explain review && rebar explain E2`; (AC2) a Python one-liner exercising `registry.explain_guide`/`explain_criterion` with a non-repo root; (AC3) `python -c "from importlib.resources import files; list(files('rebar._guides').iterdir())"`; (AC4) `ls docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md docs/passing-code-review.md`; (AC5) `pytest tests/ -k explain`.
evidence: AC1 says commands 'succeed with REBAR_ROOT pointed at a directory that has no docs/' but gives no sample shell invocation (e.g. `REBAR_ROOT=/tmp/empty rebar explain plan`).
AC2 says MCP tool and library 'succeed under the same condition' with no Python snippet or MCP call shown.
AC3 says 'present as packaged resources' with no `python -c` or pytest assertion shown.
AC4 says 'links still resolve' with no `ls` / `cat` / curl check.
AC5 says 'explain test suite pass' but names no `pytest` invocation or target.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given for any acceptance criterion; the ACs state outcomes but leave the executor to infer the exact invocation needed to verify each one, which is an E6 gap.
scenarios: An executing agent completes implementation and must decide 'am I done?' — without proving commands it must guess which commands constitute evidence of success.
CI is set up: without a named test target for the repo-root-independence assertion, the new behavior may not be exercised.
findings.suggested_fix: Append a '**Proving commands**' subsection under Acceptance criteria with the five invocations above so an agent can mechanically verify done-ness.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended pointer format in step 4 (e.g. 'a short Markdown file containing a note and relative link to the in-package source path, not a symlink') so the executor does not have to choose.
evidence: 'Leave docs/{writing-a-passing-plan,passing-code-review}.md as thin pointers to the in-package canonical source' — no format specified.
AC4 only checks that 'existing links resolve' without defining what a pointer must contain for that to be true.
findings.finding: Approach step 4 describes the docs/ files as 'thin pointers' but does not specify what form they take (symlink, a Markdown redirect note, a one-line `cat` alias), leaving the executor to choose an implementation.
scenarios: An executor who picks a symlink approach ships something that breaks on Windows or in certain wheel installs; one who picks a prose note may not satisfy the 'links resolve' AC depending on what the consumer expects.
findings.suggested_fix: Change step 4 to: 'Leave docs/{writing-a-passing-plan,passing-code-review}.md as short Markdown stubs (not symlinks) containing a one-line note: `> Canonical copy: src/rebar/_guides/<filename>.md` — preserving existing anchors and satisfying static-site link checks.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the content migration: either (a) an expand-contract sequence (keep docs/ reads as a fallback until the packaged path is validated in CI across all install modes, then remove), or (b) a stated revert procedure (e.g., 'restore originals from git tag X and revert the resolver change') so the destructive step is unambiguously reversible.
evidence: 'Move the two prose guides to src/rebar/_guides/…' — canonical location changes irreversibly for the wheel artifact
'explain_criterion(id) renders the section directly from the packaged registry … instead of reading docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md' — silent behavioral change with no revert path stated

Step 4 only covers forward compat (pointer files); no step describes how to revert if the importlib.resources resolution proves defective post-ship (e.g., certain editable-install or zip-import configurations)
No expand-contract sequencing (e.g., read from package first, fall back to docs/ temporarily, then remove fallback in a follow-up)
findings.finding: The plan performs a destructive content migration (moves canonical .md files from docs/ to src/rebar/_guides/ and changes explain_criterion to read from the packaged registry instead of docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md) but states no explicit rollback/back-out plan if the migration must be reversed.
scenarios: A release ships; a downstream install method (e.g., `pip install -e .` with an older importlib_resources shim) fails to resolve the packaged guides; the docs/ canonical files are gone and there is no documented revert procedure.
A contributor needs to roll back the commit; the pointer stubs in docs/ mean the old behavior cannot be restored by a simple git revert without re-examining which files are stubs vs. originals.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an Approach step: 'During the transition, `explain_guide` tries `importlib.resources` first and falls back to `repo_root/docs/` with a deprecation warning; the fallback is removed in the follow-up once CI confirms all install modes resolve correctly.' This is a minimal expand-contract that makes the step reversible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the three-step 'add a guide' convention (file in `_guides/`, register in `AUTHOR_GUIDES`, create `docs/` pointer) in AGENTS.md or a design doc so future contributors know the full procedure.
evidence: Plan says 'same pattern rebar already uses for criteria_routing.json' — but that pattern is not itself documented anywhere the plan points to.
AGENTS.md is mentioned in the problem context ('existing links … AGENTS.md') yet no update to it is proposed.

A future contributor adding a third guide must know: (a) drop a file in _guides/, (b) add to AUTHOR_GUIDES, (c) add a docs/ pointer — none of this convention is captured in the plan's documentation step.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new convention for how guides are authored, packaged, and registered (file in `src/rebar/_guides/`, entry in `AUTHOR_GUIDES`, thin pointer in `docs/`) but does not call for updating AGENTS.md or any design doc to document this three-step convention for future contributors.
scenarios: A contributor adds a new guide by only copying the file into _guides/ but forgets to register it in AUTHOR_GUIDES, causing a silent omission.
A contributor updates docs/ only (the old pattern) and wonders why the CLI doesn't pick it up.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step 5 to the Approach: 'Update AGENTS.md with a "Adding a new explain guide" section documenting: (1) place the .md file in `src/rebar/_guides/`, (2) add its name→filename entry to `AUTHOR_GUIDES` in the appropriate registry module, (3) add a thin pointer under `docs/` for link compatibility.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (and corresponding test) specifying the behavior when an unknown guide name is requested under the new `AUTHOR_GUIDES` resolution path (e.g., `rebar explain bogus` should return a clear error, not a KeyError/traceback).
evidence: Plan AC: 'tests updated to assert repo-root independence instead of the old "missing-file when repo has no docs" behavior' — only affirmative success cases named
Plan Approach step 2: 'AUTHOR_GUIDES maps name -> filename' — new lookup table with no specified error handling or test for unknown keys
No mention of what `rebar explain bogus-guide` returns under the new path
findings.finding: The testing plan addresses only the happy-path (explain commands succeed with no docs/) but does not specify how invalid/unknown guide names are handled under the new AUTHOR_GUIDES resolution path — a new user-facing code path with no failure-case coverage described.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — 'lint + typecheck + the explain test suite pass; tests updated to assert repo-root independence'
scenarios: User runs `rebar explain nonexistent` after install; new resolution path raises KeyError or unhelpful traceback instead of a friendly 'unknown guide' message
CI suite goes green on happy-path tests while the error branch is untested and broken
findings.suggested_fix: Add to acceptance criteria: '`rebar explain <unknown-name>` returns a user-friendly "unknown guide" error (not an unhandled exception) when the name is absent from AUTHOR_GUIDES; a test asserts this under repo-root-independent conditions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate the three new file-creation artifacts (`src/rebar/_guides/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/_guides/writing-a-passing-plan.md`, `src/rebar/_guides/passing-code-review.md`) in the plan's edit-set so the executor has a concrete checklist.
evidence: Plan: 'Move the two prose guides to `src/rebar/_guides/{writing-a-passing-plan,passing-code-review}.md` (canonical, in-package) + add `src/rebar/_guides/__init__.py`'
`list_directory('src/rebar')` shows no `_guides/` entry
No existing `src/rebar/_guides/` in repo
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_guides/` (the canonical new package directory with `__init__.py` and the two `.md` files) does not exist in the repo; the plan creates it as net-new, but the three concrete edit targets — `src/rebar/_guides/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/_guides/writing-a-passing-plan.md`, `src/rebar/_guides/passing-code-review.md` — are never listed as file-level artifacts to create, leaving the executor to infer them from step-1 prose.
scenarios: Executor omits `__init__.py` and `importlib.resources.files('rebar._guides')` raises `ModuleNotFoundError` at runtime.
Executor places files but forgets to update `AUTHOR_GUIDES` relname mapping, causing a path mismatch.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach step 1: 'Create: `src/rebar/_guides/__init__.py` (empty), `src/rebar/_guides/writing-a-passing-plan.md` (copy of current `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md`), `src/rebar/_guides/passing-code-review.md` (copy of current `docs/passing-code-review.md`).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to Approach step 5 confirming that `validate_criteria_guide` and its test callers remain repo-root-dependent by design (CI-only, not a runtime-accessible surface), to prevent an executor from inadvertently 'fixing' them.
evidence: Plan: 'Keep the dev/CI machinery (`regenerate_criteria_guide`, `validate_criteria_guide`, `_guide_path`, coach deep-links to the docs anchor) unchanged.'
`validate_criteria_guide` called in three test files outside `test_plan_review_explain.py`
These calls still resolve via `_guide_path(repo_root_path=None)` → `config.repo_root()` → project docs — fine in the rebar repo CI but not in an installed consumer
findings.finding: `validate_criteria_guide` still reads `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` via `_guide_path()` (the plan says it is unchanged), but after `explain_criterion` is changed to use `_guide_section_body` directly, `validate_criteria_guide` becomes a dev/CI-only tool that is never consulted at runtime. The plan does not acknowledge whether `validate_criteria_guide`'s callers outside `test_plan_review_explain.py` — `test_decomp_shape_criterion.py:147`, `test_necessity_criterion.py:76`, `test_asserted_capability_criterion.py:84` — remain valid after the scope change.
findings.location: Approach step 5 ('Keep the dev/CI machinery … unchanged'); tests/unit/test_decomp_shape_criterion.py:147, tests/unit/test_necessity_criterion.py:76, tests/unit/test_asserted_capability_criterion.py:84
scenarios: A consumer project whose CI runs `rebar`'s test suite (or imports and calls `validate_criteria_guide()`) gets a `missing-file` because `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is absent from the install.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Approach step 3 that `_guide_section_body` is called on the matching descriptor from `load_criteria(repo_root=repo_root_path)` (not only the packaged routing index), so project-overlay criteria are covered by the new explain_criterion path.
evidence: Plan: 'explain_criterion(id) renders the section directly from the packaged registry via the existing _guide_section_body'
Plan: 'Overlay project criteria still resolve via load_criteria(repo_root=...)'

Code: load_criteria() returns both built-in AND activated project-overlay criteria (registry.py:307-334, effective_criteria() call); _guide_section_body(criterion_dict) is a pure formatter — it works on any criterion descriptor regardless of provenance.

A project.* criterion id would appear in ids (from load_criteria) but would have no _guide_section_body output if only the packaged set is iterated, producing a spurious missing-file or silent-empty result.
findings.finding: The plan says explain_criterion 'renders the section directly from the packaged registry via the existing _guide_section_body' but uses the word 'packaged' ambiguously: an implementer may call _guide_section_body only over the built-in packaged criterion set, silently dropping project-overlay criteria (project.*) that load_criteria() correctly includes. The plan says overlay criteria 'still resolve via load_criteria(repo_root=...)' but never explicitly connects this to _guide_section_body's input set.
scenarios: A project using a project.custom-criterion in .rebar/criteria_routing.json calls `rebar explain project.custom-criterion`; the explain lookup finds the id in load_criteria(repo_root=...) but then iterates only packaged criteria for _guide_section_body, producing an unexpected error.

An implementer reads 'packaged registry' as the packaged routing index only and writes: `_guide_section_body(next(c for c in _routing_index().values() if c['id'] == criterion_id))` — which would KeyError on any overlay criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Change step 3 to: 'explain_criterion(id) looks up the matching descriptor from load_criteria(repo_root=repo_root_path) (built-ins ∪ project-overlay criteria) and renders it with _guide_section_body — eliminating the docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md file read while preserving overlay coverage.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
